headline: Caterpillar Elects New Chief Financial Officer
summary: Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE: CAT) announced today that Chief Financial Officer (CFO) Andrew Bonfield has elected to retire effective Oct. 1, 2026, following eight years with the company. Caterpillar veteran Kyle Epley has been appointed CFO effective May 1, 2026, at which time Bonfield will assume an advisory role for the remainder of the transition.

headline: Oil Prices Bounce; Iran Says Hormuz Opening Hinges On Israel, But S&P 500 Rallies
summary: Crude oil prices plunged more than $20 a barrel from Tuesday's peak after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  United Airlines, Carnival and fellow cruise and airline stocks still ranked among top S&P 500 winners in morning trade.  Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan, though they came well off their highs.

Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan, though they came well off their highs.

headline: How Ford Is Reinventing Itself as an AI Infrastructure Play After Its EV Stumble
summary: Ford (NYSE: F) closed 2025 with a $10.70 billion impairment charge tied to Model e asset write-downs and EV program cancellations, crystallizing one of the most expensive pivots in automotive history. The company emerging from that reckoning looks less like a traditional automaker and more like an industrial infrastructure play built around software, commercial fleets, and ... headline: Caterpillar Inc. Maintains Dividend
summary: The Board of Directors of Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE: CAT) voted to maintain the quarterly dividend of one dollar and fifty-one cents ($1.51) per share of common stock payable May 19, 2026, to shareholders of record at the close of business April 20, 2026.

headline: Indonesia Construction Equipment Industry Report 2026-2031: Caterpillar, Komatsu, Kobelco, Volvo CE, SANY, and Hitachi Lead Through a Diverse Set of Equipment
summary: The Indonesia Construction Equipment Market is projected to grow from 21,694 units in 2025 to 31,920 units by 2031, with a CAGR of 6.65%. Earthmoving equipment, particularly excavators, dominate the market due to Indonesia's infrastructure projects like Ibu Kota Nusantara (IKN). SANY leads with a 20%-22% market share. The market also sees rising demand for material handling and road construction equipment. Significant investments in data centers, mining, and renewable energy projects boost the s
